subject: feat(pwa): content-hash icon URLs to bust browser / CDN / SW / WebAPK caches
body:
Implements PWA_ICON_CACHE_BUST pattern. Icons referenced by stable
filenames survive "clear site data" and PWA reinstall because every
cache layer below the OS keys off URL. Adding `?v=<content-hash>` busts
4 of 5 cache layers; the 5th (OS icon cache) is surfaced to the user.

vite.config.ts:
- `iconVersion(relPath)` — sha256-8 of each icon under /public/.
  Warns (doesn't throw) on missing file so fresh clones can still
  `vite dev` before running generate-icons.
- `iconCacheBustHtml()` — Vite plugin rewrites exact-literal `href="/…"`
  on favicon.ico / favicon.png / apple-touch-icon.png to include the
  version query. Throws on missing literal so reformatted tags surface
  at build time instead of silently shipping un-versioned URLs.
- Wired BEFORE VitePWA() in the plugins array — locks the contract in.
- Manifest icons pass through `versioned()` so their src carries `?v=…`.
- workbox: ignoreURLParametersMatching: [/^v$/] so precache lookups
  still match the versioned URL (without it, offline breaks).

src/__tests__/iconCacheBust.test.ts (vitest):
- Source-level: iconCacheBustHtml wired before VitePWA; workbox has
  /^v$/; index.html still contains the literal hrefs we match.
- Dist-level (skipped when dist/ absent): manifest icons all versioned;
  index.html icon links all versioned, no bare un-versioned leaks;
  sw.js has cleanupOutdatedCaches() and /^v$/ ignore.

tsconfig.test.json:
- New config with node types, excluded from tsconfig.app.json so the
  test file's `node:fs` imports type-check without widening node types
  across the React app.

Install modal:
- Collapsed <details> "Already installed and the icon looks outdated?"
  with platform-tailored reinstall copy (iOS press-and-hold, macOS
  Dock drag, Android launcher uninstall, desktop fallback). Plain
  language; names the OS-cache limitation without jargon.

CLAUDE.md:
- Adds PWA_ICON_CACHE_BUST — implemented entry to Adopted Patterns.

Tags: pwa, infrastructure, testing, ux, documentation
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: pwa-hardening
Semver: minor
